decisions.summary: Use explicit extend declarations to choose which trait implementations become dot-call methods.
decisions.agent_behavior: Recommend extend Type with Trait::{method}; use pub extend when the attached method must be public.

claims.text: An explicit extend declaration selects which implemented trait methods are available through dot syntax.
A private extend can attach a default trait implementation to a private type for local dot calls with Self specialized to that type.
pub extend is accepted for a public type and public trait method.
The language skill causes an agent to add an explicit extend when repairing an implicit dot-call method.

decisions.summary: Using extend as an identifier warns because it is transitioning from a soft keyword to a reserved keyword.
decisions.agent_behavior: Rename identifiers named extend and reserve extend for explicit method attachment.

claims.text: Using extend as an identifier emits the soft-keyword warning at the pinned release.
Renaming the identifier passes with warnings denied.

decisions.summary: Implicit impl-to-method attachment is deprecated and is a default warning at the pinned post-release compiler build.
decisions.agent_behavior: Audit with --deny-warn, add explicit extend declarations for intended dot methods, and mark compatibility-only extend declarations deprecated when steering callers to qualified calls.

claims.text: The pinned 2026-07-15 compiler detects implicit impl-to-method attachment with the default warning set and --deny-warn.
An explicit extend declaration is the passing replacement for an intended dot-call method.
A #deprecated extend makes its compatibility dot call warn, while the qualified trait call passes as the migration target.
The language skill causes an agent to deny warnings and apply the explicit migration.

decisions.summary: Supertrait dot calls through trait objects and constrained type parameters are deprecated.
decisions.agent_behavior: Call the declaring trait explicitly as Trait::method(value) when the method comes from a supertrait or an ambiguous constraint set.

claims.text: A supertrait method dot-called through a subtrait object is detected with warnings denied.
A supertrait method dot-called through a singly constrained type parameter is detected with warnings denied.
A method dot-called through a multiply constrained type parameter is detected with warnings denied.
Calling the declaring trait in qualified form passes for all three migration cases.

decisions.summary: Or-patterns may supply defaults with with, including parenthesized defaults for multiple binders.
decisions.agent_behavior: Use Pattern(x) | (Other with x = default) to merge arms that differ only by a missing binder.

claims.text: with supplies a default for one binder missing from an or-pattern branch.
Parenthesized with defaults supply multiple missing binders.

decisions.summary: Explicit Iter literals use [| .. |] and preserve documented eager/lazy evaluation boundaries.
decisions.agent_behavior: Construct Iter values with [| .. |]; treat element expressions as eager and spread/comprehension iteration as lazy.

claims.text: The explicit [| .. |] syntax constructs an Iter.
Direct elements are eager while spread iteration and comprehension bodies are lazy.

evidence.marker: test "control flow: Iter literal evaluation order"

decisions.summary: Expected-type overloading of an array-spread literal to Iter is deprecated.
decisions.agent_behavior: Replace an overloaded [x, ..iter] expression with the explicit [| x, ..iter |] Iter literal.

claims.text: An array-spread literal overloaded to Iter emits the deprecation.
The explicit Iter literal passes under the same warning gate.

decisions.summary: Array literals support conditional spreads.
decisions.agent_behavior: Use ..if condition { values } inside an array literal when a spread is conditional.

claims.text: ..if condition { values } conditionally spreads values into an array literal.

decisions.summary: Bytes literals support UTF-8 interpolation and Bytes template writes.
decisions.agent_behavior: Use b"...\{value}" when the interpolated result should be UTF-8 Bytes, including with Buffer <+ b"..." template writes.

claims.text: A Bytes literal can interpolate a MoonBit value.
Non-ASCII interpolated text is encoded as UTF-8 Bytes.
Buffer <+ accepts a Bytes interpolation template and writes the resulting bytes.

decisions.summary: Any type may define a type-named custom constructor when the name does not conflict with an existing constructor.
decisions.agent_behavior: Define fn Type::Type(..) for a custom constructor and check that the type has no existing constructor with that name.

claims.text: A type without a conflicting constructor can define and call fn Type::Type(..).

decisions.summary: Bare empty braces are ambiguous and must be replaced with the constructor matching the intended type.
decisions.agent_behavior: Use Map([]), Json::empty_object(), Type::{}, or remove an empty block and return Unit explicitly instead of bare {}.

claims.text: Bare empty braces fail the warning gate as ambiguous.
Map([]) and Json::empty_object() are passing explicit empty collection constructors.
Type::{} is the passing explicit constructor for an empty record.
Removing the ambiguous empty block and returning Unit explicitly passes.

decisions.summary: lexscan replaces the experimental scanner and adds longest-match support.
decisions.agent_behavior: Use lexscan for regex scanning and add with longest when tokenization requires longest-match behavior.

claims.text: lexscan scans a StringView with regex cases.
lexscan with longest selects the longest of overlapping anchored matches.

decisions.summary: lexmatch is deprecated; Bytes/BytesView and guarded cases need manual rewrites because lexscan does not support them.
decisions.agent_behavior: Migrate supported cases to lexscan and explicitly redesign old Bytes or guard-dependent cases.
decisions.reason: The executable fixture proves the supported lexmatch-to-lexscan migration, but the removed Bytes/BytesView and guard forms cannot be expressed as passing lexscan cases and remain source-documented.

claims.text: lexmatch is deprecated and supported StringView cases migrate to lexscan.
Former Bytes/BytesView or guarded lexmatch cases require a manual redesign because lexscan does not support them.

evidence.marker: Documented, not executed for legacy edge cases

decisions.summary: Streaming lexscan support is a future announcement, not current behavior.
decisions.reason: The release explicitly describes this as future work, so the current skills must not claim or teach it.

decisions.summary: Legacy JSON build configuration is deprecated and moon fmt migrates it to the DSL files.
decisions.agent_behavior: Do not create moon.mod.json or moon.pkg.json; migrate existing files with moon fmt and review the deletion.

claims.text: The legacy JSON files are accepted as the deprecated input to the migration command.
moon fmt creates the replacement DSL files and removes the legacy JSON files.

decisions.summary: The @ warning switch is planned for deprecation in favor of moon --deny-warn.
decisions.agent_behavior: Use +name or -name for warning selection and enforce warnings in CI with --deny-warn instead of @name.
decisions.reason: The release announces a future deprecation rather than a diagnostic available at the 0.10.4 pin, so both sides of the migration are documented from the pinned source instead of claimed executable.

claims.text: The @ warning switch is the deprecated-form side of the announced migration.
Warning selection should use +name/-name and CI error promotion should use moon --deny-warn.

decisions.summary: The 0.10.4 native backend platform matrix, defaults, overrides, and release-build strategy vary by OS and channel.
decisions.agent_behavior: Check OS, architecture, channel, build mode, and MOONBIT_NEW_NATIVE before diagnosing which native backend is active.

claims.text: The native-backend availability, default selection, release-build strategy, and override depend on the documented 0.10.4 platform matrix.

evidence.marker: Documented, not executed across the full platform matrix

decisions.summary: moon.pkg supports pkgtype and foreign-library functions support #export_name.
decisions.agent_behavior: Use pkgtype(kind: ...) for package type and #export_name only on functions declared by a foreign_library package.

claims.text: moon.pkg accepts the new pkgtype(kind: ...) declaration.
A foreign_library package accepts #export_name on its function.

decisions.summary: #export_name export scope and native library maturity have backend-specific limits.
decisions.agent_behavior: Expect exported symbols only from the foreign-library package itself and prefer Wasm/JS at the 0.10.4 pin.

claims.text: #export_name only exports functions from the foreign-library package itself, and native library output remains less mature than Wasm/JS at this pin.

decisions.summary: source and formatter are top-level configuration declarations.
decisions.agent_behavior: Write source in moon.mod and formatter(ignore: ...) in moon.pkg at top level rather than hiding them in options.

claims.text: moon.mod accepts top-level source and moon.pkg accepts top-level formatter(ignore: ...).

decisions.summary: The release fixes unspecified LSP crashes and intermittent failures.
decisions.reason: No stable command, configuration, or troubleshooting rule is specified; claiming more would invent behavior beyond the release note.

decisions.summary: This is a heading for separately assessed moon behavior changes.
decisions.reason: The heading contains no behavior beyond its five child items, each of which has its own decision.

decisions.summary: Tests and pre-build steps now run from the module root and pre-build paths are relative to it.
decisions.agent_behavior: Resolve test and pre-build paths from the module root, not the nested package directory.

claims.text: Test and pre-build working directories and pre-build relative paths use the module root at this release.

evidence.marker: Documented, not executed from a nested package

decisions.summary: macOS watch mode moved to FSEvents to fix crashes.
decisions.reason: The command and user workflow are unchanged; the implementation fix creates no durable skill guidance.

decisions.summary: moon run propagates the executed program's exit code.
decisions.agent_behavior: Treat moon run's exit status as the program exit status in scripts and CI.

claims.text: moon run returns the nonzero exit code produced by the executed program.

decisions.summary: moon bench accepts path filtering.
decisions.agent_behavior: Pass a package or file path positionally to moon bench to narrow benchmark scope.

claims.text: moon bench accepts a positional package or file path filter.

decisions.summary: Workspace-level preferred-target is deprecated and moon run uses the module's value.
decisions.agent_behavior: Place preferred_target in each module and do not recommend a workspace-level setting.
decisions.reason: The repository has no multi-module workspace fixture for the old key; the pinned release and explicitly labeled reference document both the removed placement and module-level replacement.

claims.text: The workspace-level preferred-target placement is deprecated.
preferred_target belongs in each module and moon run respects the module value.

evidence.marker: put the setting in each module

decisions.summary: The Mooncakes skills marketplace accepts page URLs through npx skills.
decisions.reason: This is an external skill-distribution marketplace behavior, not MoonBit language semantics or moon project operation covered by the two product skills.

decisions.summary: Parser error recovery improved without a specified syntax or diagnostic contract.
decisions.reason: The release gives no stable diagnostic or recovery behavior that a user-facing skill can safely promise.

decisions.summary: moonbitlang/async 0.20.2 adds an experimental Wasm1 runtime with explicit compatibility and API limits.
decisions.agent_behavior: Distinguish Wasm1 from wasm-gc, require the latest moonrun for async Wasm1 artifacts, and check the documented unsupported APIs before recommending that backend.

claims.text: moonbitlang/async 0.20.2 adds experimental Wasm1 support with native-compatible API signatures and behavior.
Its Wasm1 artifacts require the latest moonrun and do not yet run in other WebAssembly runtimes.
The same artifact is cross-platform wherever the required moonrun is available.
Backward compatibility for artifacts built with older async package versions is not promised yet.
The experimental backend does not yet support @websocket, @fs.Watcher, or @fs.realpath, and their support is future work.

decisions.summary: moonbitlang/async 0.20.2 changes package-specific filesystem, I/O, and Windows APIs.
decisions.reason: The language skill now documents the async runtime boundary that affects whether language-level async code runs, but it does not catalogue the versioned filesystem, I/O, stdio, symlink, or Windows API surface of the external moonbitlang/async package; those calls must follow the installed package documentation.

decisions.summary: This is a heading for separately assessed moonbitlang/core changes.
decisions.reason: The heading contains no behavior beyond the seven child items, each of which has its own decision.

decisions.summary: Core added experimental SIMD and internal performance improvements.
decisions.reason: These optimizations do not change source syntax, API selection, or a stable user workflow the skills need to teach.

decisions.summary: Immutable containers use type-named constructors and deprecate from_array.
decisions.agent_behavior: Generate @package.Type([...]) for immutable containers and migrate Type::from_array calls.

claims.text: The immutable-container Type::from_array constructor emits a deprecation.
The type-named immutable-container constructor passes as the replacement.
The language skill causes an agent to replace from_array without incorrectly changing mutable Set construction.

decisions.summary: The moonbitlang/core argparse package improved subcommand parsing.
decisions.reason: The product skills do not catalogue APIs of optional core packages; this is not the moon CLI parser and should be discovered from the installed argparse package docs.

decisions.summary: Core added Json::empty_object, String predicates and code-unit search, and Int16/UInt16 lnot.
decisions.agent_behavior: Use these current core APIs when their exact behavior fits instead of recreating helpers or writing ambiguous empty JSON braces.

claims.text: Json::empty_object constructs an empty JSON object.
String all/any predicates and contains_code_unit execute with their documented behavior.
Int16 and UInt16 lnot execute with fixed-width complement behavior.

decisions.summary: View operators no longer accept negative indices.
decisions.agent_behavior: Compute a nonnegative index explicitly before slicing a StringView, BytesView, or ArrayView.

claims.text: Negative ArrayView, StringView, and BytesView indices trigger the observed failure, and explicit nonnegative indices are passing replacements.

decisions.summary: Deprecated collection aliases and IterResult were removed.
decisions.agent_behavior: Do not generate collection #alias(T) APIs or IterResult; use current constructors and Iter APIs from the installed core library.

claims.text: Collection #alias(T) APIs and IterResult are removed and must not be generated at this release.

evidence.marker: Documented, not executed as removal diagnostics

decisions.summary: Core environment APIs fixed Unicode handling on Windows.
decisions.reason: The API and recommended usage are unchanged; the platform bug fix creates no durable skill instruction.
